findings.evidence: roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md:128-131 names the finite-dimensional comodule category; code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Finite.lean:61-65 defines `FGComoduleCat`; grep finds no `HasZeroObject (FGComoduleCat ...)` or `FGComoduleCat.zero`.
findings.fix: Add the finite zero object API, e.g. prove `(ComoduleCat.isFG R C).ContainsZero` or define `FGComoduleCat.zero`/`HasZeroObject`, using `Module.Finite R PUnit`.
findings.issue: The zero-object instance is only for `ComoduleCat`, but the roadmap-facing category is the finite-dimensional/finitely generated subcategory `FGComoduleCat`; downstream code still has no `HasZeroObject (FGComoduleCat R C)` API.

findings.evidence: TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/Comodule/Zero.lean:106 and :112 define plain theorems; existing zero morphism normal forms such as `ComoduleCat.zero_comp`/`comp_zero` are `[simp]` at ComoduleCat.lean:291 and :298.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to both lemmas, or provide separate `[simp]` normal-form lemmas rewriting such morphisms to `0`.
findings.issue: `zero_hom_ext` and `hom_zero_ext` are the characteristic normal-form lemmas for maps out of/into the zero comodule, but they are not marked for simplification.

summary: The public surface does not yet cover the finite-dimensional/finitely generated comodule category named by the roadmap target, and the new zero-object morphism normal forms are not exposed to automation.
